author: Dr. Lizard
title: Which exclusion criteria prevent diagnosis of MDS-SF3B1 in both ICC and WHO-5?
answer: Presence of del(5q), monosomy 7, or complex karyotype excludes MDS-SF3B1 diagnosis in both systems.
detail: These cytogenetic abnormalities indicate higher-risk disease that supersedes the SF3B1-defined category.
Del(5q) cases are classified separately as MDS with isolated del(5q) if they meet specific criteria.
Monosomy 7 and complex karyotype indicate poor prognosis that overrides favorable SF3B1 biology.
Both systems require checking cytogenetics before assigning the MDS-SF3B1 diagnosis.
releaseNote: Exclusion criteria for MDS-SF3B1 diagnosis in ICC and WHO-5 include del(5q), monosomy 7, and complex karyotype, which indicate higher-risk disease or poor prognosis.
